Drafthaus
End User Agreement
Please read and accept before continuing.

Last updated: April 2026

By using Drafthaus ("the Service"), you agree to the following terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the Service.

1. Acceptance of Terms

By accessing or using Drafthaus, you confirm that you are at least 13 years of age and agree to be bound by this End User Agreement. If you are under 18, you represent that you have your parent or guardian's consent.

2. Your Content & Intellectual Property

You retain full ownership of all songs, recordings, lyrics, and other creative content you create or upload to Drafthaus. We do not claim any rights to your content. You grant Drafthaus a limited license solely to store, process, and deliver your content back to you as part of the Service.

3. Data Storage & Loss

Drafthaus is not responsible for any lost, stolen, corrupted, or otherwise inaccessible data. While we make reasonable efforts to safeguard your content, the Service is provided on an "as-is" basis. You are solely responsible for maintaining your own backups of any recordings, lyrics, or other materials you consider important. Server failures, software bugs, account issues, or other events may result in data loss, and Drafthaus shall not be held liable.

4. Acceptable Use

You agree not to use Drafthaus to store, distribute, or create content that is illegal, infringes on third-party rights, or violates any applicable laws. You will not attempt to reverse-engineer, exploit, or interfere with the operation of the Service.

5. Account & Security

You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials. Drafthaus is not liable for unauthorized access to your account resulting from your failure to protect your login information.

6. Service Availability

Drafthaus may experience downtime for maintenance, updates, or unforeseen issues. We do not guarantee uninterrupted access to the Service. Features may be added, modified, or removed at any time.

7. Subscriptions & Payments

Certain features require a paid subscription. Payments are processed through Stripe. Subscription terms, pricing, and refund policies are presented at the time of purchase. Free-tier limitations apply as described within the app.

8. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Drafthaus and its creator shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of data, revenue, or creative work, arising from your use of or inability to use the Service.

9. Termination

We reserve the right to suspend or terminate your access to Drafthaus at any time, with or without cause. Upon termination, your right to use the Service ceases immediately. We recommend exporting your content regularly.

10. Changes to This Agreement

We may update this agreement from time to time. Continued use of Drafthaus after changes are posted constitutes your acceptance of the revised terms.

11. Privacy

Your use of Drafthaus is also governed by our Privacy Policy, which describes how we collect, use, and protect your data.

12. Contact

For questions about this agreement, contact us at hellojasoncraig@gmail.com.

Drafthaus
Privacy Policy
How we handle your data.

Last updated: April 2026

Drafthaus ("we", "us", "the Service") is committed to protecting your privacy. This policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and your rights regarding your data.

1. Information We Collect

Account Information — When you sign up, we collect your email address and display name via Firebase Authentication. If you sign in with Google, we receive your name, email, and profile photo from Google. Guest users are assigned an anonymous session ID with no personal information attached.

Your Content — Songs, lyrics, chord progressions, audio recordings (takes), loops, and any other creative content you create or upload. This content is stored in Google Cloud (Firebase Firestore and Firebase Storage) and is associated with your account.

Usage Data — We collect basic usage information such as subscription tier, storage quota usage, and feature interactions to maintain and improve the Service. We do not use third-party analytics or tracking services.

Device & Browser Data — We may collect browser type, operating system, and screen size for debugging and compatibility purposes. This data is not linked to your identity.

2. How We Use Your Information

We use your information solely to provide, maintain, and improve Drafthaus. Specifically: to authenticate your identity, store and sync your songs across devices, process subscription payments through Stripe, enforce usage quotas, and diagnose technical issues.

3. Data Storage & Security

Your data is stored on Google Cloud infrastructure (Firebase) in accordance with Google's security standards, including encryption at rest and in transit. Audio recordings are stored in Firebase Storage. While we take reasonable measures to protect your data, no system is 100% secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.

4. We Do Not Sell Your Data

Drafthaus does not sell, rent, trade, or share your personal information or creative content with third parties for marketing or advertising purposes. Your songs and recordings are yours.

5. Third-Party Services

We use the following third-party services to operate Drafthaus:

Firebase (Google) — Authentication, database, file storage, and hosting.
Stripe — Payment processing for subscriptions. Stripe handles all payment data directly; we never see or store your full credit card number.

Each of these services has their own privacy policies governing how they handle data.

6. Cookies & Local Storage

Drafthaus uses browser localStorage to save your preferences (theme, input settings, EULA acceptance) and to cache data for offline performance. We do not use tracking cookies or third-party advertising cookies.

7. Your Rights

You have the right to:

Access — View all data associated with your account within the app.
Export — Export your songs, recordings, and lyrics using the built-in export features.
Delete — Delete individual songs, recordings, or your entire account. Deleted content is moved to trash and permanently removed after the retention period.
Correction — Update your account information at any time.

To request full account deletion or a copy of all your data, contact us at hellojasoncraig@gmail.com.

8. Children's Privacy

Drafthaus is not intended for children under 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13. If you believe a child under 13 has provided us with personal data, please contact us and we will delete it promptly.

9. Data Retention

We retain your data for as long as your account is active. Deleted content is moved to trash and permanently purged after the configured retention period. If you delete your account, all associated data is removed from our systems within 30 days.

10. Changes to This Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Changes will be reflected by the "Last updated" date above. Continued use of Drafthaus after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

11. Contact

For privacy-related questions or requests, contact us at hellojasoncraig@gmail.com.

About
Drafthaus
Built by Jason Craig: songwriter, producer, perpetual work-in-progress.
Singer / Guitarist Producer Music Production, Toronto Film School BA Psychology VP, CTS & Associates Toronto Area Director, Rockstar Music Central

I've been writing, performing, and recording music since I was 13, tinkering away in my parents' house with whatever I could get my hands on. Over the years I played in bands, landed as a singer and guitarist in Iduna, and still show up every Thursday night to write with those guys, make noise, and catch up. Some things don't change.

Along the way I picked up a BA in psychology, went to Toronto Film School for music production, and made my way through pretty much every recording setup you've heard of: a Tascam 4-track cassette when I was just getting started, Audacity when I got my first computer, then Nuendo, Cubase, Pro Tools, and finally Logic, where I've stayed. By day I work as VP of Client Services and Strategic Partnerships at CTS & Associates, and as Toronto Area Director at Rockstar Music Central, focused on growth, partnerships, and teaching music to the next generation of players.

Music has been a constant through all of it. But here's the honest problem: I have a million unfinished Logic projects. Ideas, fragments, sketches that never went anywhere. Not because they weren't good, but because the moment I opened a DAW I was already thinking about production instead of the song.

That's why I built Drafthaus.

I needed a place, anywhere, any device, to just write. Put the idea down, work out the chords, lyrics, and structure without the pressure of a full session staring back at me. And when something was actually ready, export it: MIDI, audio notes, chord charts, and bring it into Logic with something real to work from.

Drafthaus is that place. It's a songwriter's workbench, not a DAW. The draft comes first. Everything else comes after. I hope it's as useful to you as it's been to me keeping all my ideas organized.

Made with too many late nights & not enough finished songs. Jason

Bluetooth Sync
Trim latency on save
Offset ms
Removes the first N ms from each recording to compensate for Bluetooth input latency.
Place your Bluetooth mic near your speaker. Keep the room quiet. The app will play 5 clicks and measure how long they take to reach the mic.

Welcome to Drafthaus
1Tap +New to start blank, or Roll to get a generated beat + chords
2Assign recordings, loops, beats & chords to each song part
